title: Standing report (structured mirror)
description: Machine-parseable mirror of the six-field standing report format documented in CLAUDE.md's Reporting convention. The prose report on its branch is the record of truth for a human reader; this is a parity artifact for tooling that needs to route on verdict/deviations/open-items without re-parsing prose. Tiered: `summary` is read on every report; `detail` is read only when summary signals something needs it.

task.description: Field 1 of the standing format: name + branch(es) + PR/commit refs.

refs.description: PR numbers / commit SHAs this report is about.

summary.description: Always read. Fields 2, 3, and 5 of the standing format, condensed: what happened, what diverged, what needs a decision. Most reports are clean and this tier is the whole read.

verdict.description: clean = shipped as directed, no deviations, no open items. deviations = shipped with noted departures from spec. blocked = nothing or partial shipped.

shipped.description: Per numbered item of the instruction executed. Empty + verdict:blocked means nothing shipped.

deviations.description: Omit or empty array means "none" — matches the prose format's explicit 'none' rather than a missing field.

answerable.description: True if a single reply resolves it (per the standing format's 'numbered, answerable' rule).

detail.description: Read only when summary.verdict != 'clean', or summary.open_items is non-empty. Fields 4 and 6 of the standing format. A directive referencing this report cites `report_path` + an item number here — never re-quotes its content.

deferred_reason.description: Set instead of result when this check was deferred rather than run.

live_state.description: Anything running, deployed, pushed, or left behind.

report_path.description: Repo-relative path to the full prose report (docs/reports/<date>-<topic>.md) when one was committed. Directives referencing this report cite this path + an item number; they do not re-paste content from it.
